text.name: ads_maintenancePrice_tooltip
text.text: Adjusts the cost of all workshop services, including inspections, repairs, and overhauls. A higher value makes all maintenance activities more expensive.

text.name: ads_maintenanceDuration_tooltip
text.text: Adjusts the duration of all workshop services. A higher value increases the time your vehicle will spend undergoing maintenance or repairs.

text.name: ads_workshopOpenHour_tooltip
text.text: The hour of the day when the workshop opens. Repairs are only active during work hours.

text.name: ads_workshopCloseHour_tooltip
text.text: The hour of the day when the workshop closes. Repairs will pause after this time.

text.name: ads_engineMaxHeat_tooltip
text.text: The base rate of heat generated by the engine at maximum load. Higher values will cause the engine to heat up faster.

text.name: ads_cloggingSpeed_tooltip
text.text: Adjusts how quickly the radiator and air intake clog while working in dust, chaff, and crop residue. Higher values make clogging build up faster. At 100%, the clogging rate is roughly one third of the service interval.

text.name: ads_instantInspection_tooltip
text.text: When enabled, all inspections complete instantly (no time passes). Note: the 'Visual' inspection option will become unavailable

text.name: ads_parkVehicle_tooltip
text.text: If enabled, vehicles undergoing maintenance are skipped when cycling with TAB.

text.name: ads_warrantyEnabled_tooltip
text.text: Applies warranty coverage to repairs on new vehicles (up to 20 operating hours or 12 months). Only OEM parts are covered at no cost.

text.name: ads_aiOverloadAndOverheatControl_tooltip
text.text: If enabled, the AI worker reduces load to prevent engine overload or overheating.

text.name: ads_debugMode_tooltip
text.text: Enables or disables debug panel and extensive debug logging in the console.

text.name: ads_warningMessages_tooltip
text.text: Enables or disables gameplay warning messages such as cold engine and overheating alerts.

text.name: ads_serviceInterval_tooltip
text.text: Sets the base service interval in hours after which maintenance is required. The actual interval varies depending on the vehicle brand, age, and quality of previous maintenance.

text.name: ads_vehicleLifespan_tooltip
text.text: Sets the base vehicle lifespan in hours, after which its condition will reach 0%. The closer the condition is to zero, the more unreliable the vehicle becomes.

text.name: ads_downtimeWear_tooltip
text.text: Sets the passive wear rate while a vehicle is idle as a percentage of the base wear rate (Vehicle Lifespan). For example, 5% with the default 100-hour lifespan means each system loses about 1% condition after roughly 20 hours of downtime. Note: passive wear is disabled while the vehicle is stored under cover.

text.name: ads_generalWearEnabled_tooltip
text.text: Enables age-related wear. As the vehicle wears out, each system receives small penalties to its core characteristics, such as engine power, hydraulic speed, or brake efficiency. The effect becomes active after the first third of the vehicle lifespan, but only becomes noticeable after the second third and grows stronger toward the end of the machine's service life.

text.name: ads_mobileWorkshopRestrictions_tooltip
text.text: When enabled, the availability of different procedures in the mobile workshop depends on the vehicle's maintainability.

text.name: ads_systemStressRate_tooltip
text.text: Controls how quickly stress builds up in systems from overload, overheating, wheel slip, and other harsh operating conditions. Stress directly affects breakdown probability: the more stress a system accumulates, the more likely it is to fail.

text.name: ads_batteryCapacity_tooltip
text.text: Adjusts the maximum usable battery capacity. Values close to 150 Ah are more realistic, but you may barely encounter battery discharge problems at that level.

text.name: ads_thermalSensitivity_tooltip
text.text: Adjusts the thermal model behavior. Higher settings make the engine and transmission more sensitive to load, ambient temperature, and radiator fouling — generating more heat under the same conditions.

text.name: ads_ws_info_no_breakdowns
text.text: There are no breakdowns or they have not yet been detected.

text.name: ads_ws_cancel_confirm_text
text.text: Current progress will be saved as-is. No money will be refunded.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_general_wear_stage1_description
text.text: The accumulated stress of years of hard work. Minor, irreversible wear on core components slightly reduces overall performance.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_defected_parts_detected_description
text.text: The unit is functioning normally, but diagnostics have revealed a defective part. Due to a critically low safety margin, preventive replacement is recommended to avoid sudden failure.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_temporarily_repaired_description
text.text: The unit is functioning normally, but relies entirely on a quick repair of the worn elements. To avoid an inevitable breakdown under load, a full replacement of the parts is recommended as soon as possible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_overheat_protection_stage1_message
text.text: Engine limp mode active! Power reduced!

text.name: ads_breakdowns_overheat_protection_stage2_message
text.text: Engine limp mode active! Power reduced!

text.name: ads_breakdowns_overheat_protection_stage3_message
text.text: Engine limp mode active! Power reduced!

text.name: ads_breakdowns_overheat_protection_stage4_message
text.text: Engine is turned off to protect from overheating

text.name: ads_breakdowns_ecu_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: ECU errors. Slightly reduced engine power.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_ecu_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: ECU malfunction. Noticeable power loss and increased fuel consumption.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_ecu_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Critical ECU malfunction. Significant power loss, high fuel consumption, and risk of engine stalls.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_ecu_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Complete ECU failure. The engine cannot be controlled and will not start.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_corroded_wiring_stage1_description
text.text: Corrosion of wiring and electrical connectors. Increased contact resistance causes occasional light flicker, unstable voltage supply, and difficult engine starting.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_corroded_wiring_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate corrosion of wiring and electrical connectors. Contact losses cause frequent light flicker, unstable voltage supply, difficult engine starting, and possible engine stalls.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_corroded_wiring_stage3_description
text.text: Severe corrosion of wiring and electrical connectors. Power supply to critical circuits is unstable, lighting may fail completely, engine torque is reduced, engine stalls are likely, and starting is unreliable.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_corroded_wiring_stage4_description
text.text: Failure of the starter control circuit caused by severe wiring corrosion. Lighting is inoperative and engine start is impossible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_battery_sulfation_stage1_description
text.text: Battery sulfation. Effective battery capacity is reduced, resulting in lower electrical reserve under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_battery_sulfation_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate battery sulfation. Effective battery capacity is significantly reduced and engine starting is difficult.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_battery_sulfation_stage3_description
text.text: Severe battery sulfation. Usable battery capacity is critically low and cranking performance is unstable.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_battery_sulfation_stage4_description
text.text: Critical battery sulfation. Effective battery capacity is almost absent and cranking performance is insufficient for reliable engine start.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_alternator_regulator_failure_stage1_description
text.text: Alternator regulator malfunction. Charging output is reduced, resulting in lower battery charging efficiency.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_alternator_regulator_failure_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate alternator regulator malfunction. Charging output is unstable and reduced, causing poor battery charging and increased voltage losses in the electrical system.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_alternator_regulator_failure_stage3_description
text.text: Severe alternator regulator malfunction. Charging output is critically reduced, voltage supply is unstable, and the electrical system operates with major losses.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_alternator_regulator_failure_stage4_description
text.text: Complete alternator regulator failure. Battery charging is absent and the electrical system is operating only from the remaining battery reserve.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_turbocharger_wear_stage1_description
text.text: Worn turbo bearings. A faint whistling sound can be heard, and there's a slight loss of power at high RPM.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_turbocharger_wear_stage2_description
text.text: Leaking turbo seals. Noticeable power loss, increased fuel consumption.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_turbocharger_wear_stage3_description
text.text: Damaged turbine blades. Severe power loss and a risk of engine stalls under heavy load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_turbocharger_wear_stage4_description
text.text: Catastrophic turbo failure. The engine has lost most of its power and is in danger of severe internal damage.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_oil_pump_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Oil pump malfunction. Oil pressure is reduced, engine torque is slightly decreased, engine temperature is elevated, and mechanical knocking may be present.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_oil_pump_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate oil pump malfunction. Oil pressure is insufficient, engine torque is noticeably reduced, engine temperature is increased, and mechanical knocking is clearly audible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_oil_pump_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Severe oil pump malfunction. Oil pressure is critically low, engine torque is heavily reduced, engine overheating is likely, mechanical knocking is severe, and engine stalls may occur.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_oil_pump_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Critical oil pump failure. Oil circulation is insufficient for safe engine operation and engine start or further operation is not possible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_valve_train_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Valve train malfunction. Engine torque is slightly reduced, fuel consumption is increased, and abnormal valve train noise is present.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_valve_train_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate valve train malfunction. Engine torque is noticeably reduced, fuel consumption is increased, valve train noise is pronounced, and engine hesitation may occur under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_valve_train_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Severe valve train malfunction. Engine torque is heavily reduced, fuel consumption is significantly increased, valve train noise is severe, engine hesitation is frequent, and engine stalls may occur.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_valve_train_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Critical valve train failure. Correct valve operation is no longer ensured and engine start or further operation is not possible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_pump_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Early fuel pump wear. Slightly reduced engine power and increased fuel consumption.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_pump_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Fuel pressure dropping. Noticeable power loss, higher fuel consumption, and occasional engine stalls.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_pump_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Critical fuel pressure. Severe power loss, very high fuel consumption, and frequent engine stalls.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_pump_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Complete fuel pump failure. Fuel is not supplied to the engine.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_injector_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: One or more injectors have slightly clogged nozzles. The engine may run a bit rough, with a minor loss of power.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_injector_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Poor fuel atomization. The engine misfires under load, resulting in noticeable power loss and engine hesitation.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_injector_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: A fuel injector is sticking. The engine runs very poorly, with significant power loss and unreliable starting.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_injector_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: An injector has completely failed. The engine will not run correctly and may not start at all.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_brake_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Worn brake pads. A slight decrease in braking effectiveness.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_brake_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Leaking brake fluid or worn discs. Braking distance is noticeably increased.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_brake_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Severe loss of brake pressure. Brakes are very weak and unreliable.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_brake_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Complete brake system failure. Further operation is extremely dangerous.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_transmission_slip_stage1_description
text.text: Worn clutch plates. Occasional power loss due to transmission slipping under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_transmission_slip_stage2_description
text.text: Significant clutch wear. Noticeable transmission slipping, especially during acceleration.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_transmission_slip_stage3_description
text.text: Heavily worn clutch. Severe slipping makes it difficult to transfer power to the wheels.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_transmission_slip_stage4_description
text.text: Clutch completely burned out. The vehicle is unable to move.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_transmission_synchronizer_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Worn synchronizers. Occasional grinding or difficulty when shifting gears.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_transmission_synchronizer_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Damaged synchronizers. Shifting gears is frequently difficult and may fail.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_transmission_synchronizer_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Broken synchronizer teeth. High chance of failed shifts or gear rejection under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_transmission_synchronizer_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Synchronizers completely failed. It is impossible to shift gears.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_powershift_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Low hydraulic pressure in Powershift. Slight delay and harshness when engaging gears.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_powershift_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Worn hydraulic pump seals. Noticeable lag and jolts during gear shifts.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_powershift_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Failing hydraulic pump. Severe delay and violent shocks when shifting.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_powershift_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Powershift hydraulic pump failed. Transmission is stuck in neutral.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_cvt_chain_wear_stage1_description
text.text: CVT chain wear. Chain slip is present and transmission heat generation is increased.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_cvt_chain_wear_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate CVT chain wear. Chain slip is noticeable, fuel consumption is increased, and transmission operating temperature is elevated.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_cvt_chain_wear_stage3_description
text.text: Severe CVT chain wear. Chain slip is pronounced, fuel consumption is significantly increased, and transmission overheating is likely.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_cvt_chain_wear_stage4_description
text.text: Critical CVT chain wear. Torque transmission through the variator is no longer reliable and vehicle movement cannot be ensured.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_control_valve_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: CVT hydraulic control valve malfunction. Hydraulic pressure drops occur intermittently, engine torque is slightly reduced, and the maximum transmission ratio range is limited.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_control_valve_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate CVT hydraulic control valve malfunction. Hydraulic pressure instability is frequent, engine torque is reduced, transmission temperature is increased, and the available ratio range is further limited.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_control_valve_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Severe CVT hydraulic control valve malfunction. Hydraulic pressure drops are pronounced, engine torque is heavily reduced, transmission overheating is likely, and ratio range limitation is significant.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_control_valve_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Critical CVT hydraulic control valve failure. Pressure control is no longer stable, the ratio range is severely restricted, transmission heating is excessive, and the powertrain operates in emergency mode.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_cvt_addon_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Early signs of a CVT transmission malfunction have been detected. Inspection and repair are required.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_cvt_addon_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: The CVT transmission malfunction is getting worse. Continued operation may lead to emergency mode.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_cvt_addon_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Critical CVT transmission damage. The transmission is in emergency mode and the maximum vehicle speed is reduced.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_cvt_addon_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Complete CVT transmission failure. Further operation is impossible until the transmission is repaired.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_thermostat_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Engine thermostat is beginning to fail. The engine takes longer to reach optimal temperature or runs slightly hot.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_thermostat_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Thermostat response is poor. Engine temperature regulation is unstable, increasing wear.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_thermostat_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: The thermostat is stuck closed. The engine will constantly overheat under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_thermostat_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Complete thermostat failure. The engine coolant cannot circulate correctly, leading to imminent and severe overheating.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Minor internal pump leak. Hydraulic implements are slightly slower.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Worn pump seals. Noticeably slower hydraulic operations.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Damaged hydraulic pump. Implements are extremely slow and weak.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Hydraulic pump failure. Hydraulic system is inoperable.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_cylinder_internal_leak_stage1_description
text.text: Internal leakage in a hydraulic cylinder. Hydraulic actuation speed is slightly reduced and attached implements may drift slowly under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_cylinder_internal_leak_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate internal leakage in a hydraulic cylinder. Hydraulic actuation speed is noticeably reduced and load holding stability is impaired.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_cylinder_internal_leak_stage3_description
text.text: Severe internal leakage in a hydraulic cylinder. Hydraulic actuation is slow and weak, and attached implements cannot reliably hold position under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_hydraulic_cylinder_internal_leak_stage4_description
text.text: Critical internal leakage in a hydraulic cylinder. Hydraulic movement is almost lost and load holding is no longer possible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_pto_clutch_slip_stage1_description
text.text: PTO clutch slip. Torque transmission through the PTO is reduced and the PTO may disengage under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_pto_clutch_slip_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate PTO clutch slip. Torque transmission through the PTO is significantly reduced and automatic PTO disengagement under load occurs frequently.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_pto_clutch_slip_stage3_description
text.text: Severe PTO clutch slip. Usable PTO torque is heavily reduced and stable operation of PTO-driven equipment cannot be ensured.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_pto_clutch_slip_stage4_description
text.text: Critical PTO clutch failure. Torque transmission through the PTO is lost and PTO operation is not possible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_bearing_wear_stage1_description
text.text: Wheel hub bearing wear. Bearing noise and vibration are present, maximum speed is slightly limited, and rolling resistance is increased.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_bearing_wear_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate wheel hub bearing wear. Bearing noise and vibration are pronounced, maximum speed is reduced, and drivetrain load is increased.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_bearing_wear_stage3_description
text.text: Severe wheel hub bearing wear. Vibration is strong, maximum speed is significantly reduced, drivetrain load is elevated, and seizure noise may occur.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_bearing_wear_stage4_description
text.text: Critical wheel hub bearing failure. The bearing has seized and wheel rotation is blocked.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_steering_linkage_wear_stage1_description
text.text: Steering linkage wear. A slight steering pull is present and steering response is reduced.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_steering_linkage_wear_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate steering linkage wear. Steering pull is noticeable and steering response is significantly reduced.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_steering_linkage_wear_stage3_description
text.text: Severe steering linkage wear. Directional stability is impaired, steering pull is pronounced, and steering response is heavily reduced.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_steering_linkage_wear_stage4_description
text.text: Critical steering linkage failure. Steering response is almost lost and safe directional control cannot be ensured.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_track_tensioner_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Track tensioner malfunction. Directional pull, vibration, drag noise, and a slight reduction in maximum speed are present.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_track_tensioner_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate track tensioner malfunction. Directional instability and vibration are noticeable, traction resistance is increased, engine hesitation may occur, and maximum speed is reduced.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_track_tensioner_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Severe track tensioner malfunction. Directional instability is pronounced, vibration and drag noise are severe, drivetrain load is increased, and maximum speed is significantly reduced.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_track_tensioner_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Critical track tensioner failure. Track running is no longer stable and seizure of the running gear has occurred.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_coolant_leak_stage1_description
text.text: Coolant leak. Cooling system efficiency is slightly reduced.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_coolant_leak_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate coolant leak. Cooling system efficiency is reduced and engine operating temperature rises more quickly under load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_coolant_leak_stage3_description
text.text: Severe coolant leak. Cooling system efficiency is significantly reduced and overheating under load is likely.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_coolant_leak_stage4_description
text.text: Critical coolant leak. Cooling system efficiency is critically reduced and safe engine temperature cannot be maintained.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fun_clutch_failure_stage1_description
text.text: Fan clutch malfunction. Cooling fan efficiency is slightly reduced and abnormal fan clutch noise is present.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fun_clutch_failure_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate fan clutch malfunction. Cooling fan efficiency is reduced and fan clutch noise is clearly audible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fun_clutch_failure_stage3_description
text.text: Severe fan clutch malfunction. Cooling fan efficiency is significantly reduced and fan clutch noise is severe.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fun_clutch_failure_stage4_description
text.text: Critical fan clutch failure. Cooling fan efficiency is critically reduced and adequate airflow through the cooling system cannot be ensured.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_filter_clogging_stage1_description
text.text: Fuel filter clogging. Fuel flow is restricted, causing slight engine hesitation under load and a minor reduction in engine torque.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_filter_clogging_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate fuel filter clogging. Fuel flow restriction is noticeable, engine hesitation under load is frequent, engine torque is reduced, and engine stalls may occur.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_filter_clogging_stage3_description
text.text: Severe fuel filter clogging. Fuel flow is critically restricted, engine hesitation is pronounced, engine torque is heavily reduced, and engine stalls are likely.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_filter_clogging_stage4_description
text.text: Critical fuel filter clogging. Fuel flow through the filter is insufficient for engine operation and engine start is not possible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_line_air_leak_stage1_description
text.text: Air leak in the fuel line. Fuel supply loses stability and engine starting becomes more difficult.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_line_air_leak_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate air leak in the fuel line. Fuel supply is unstable, engine hesitation is present, engine starting is difficult, and engine stalls may occur.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_line_air_leak_stage3_description
text.text: Severe air leak in the fuel line. Fuel supply continuity is disrupted, engine hesitation is pronounced, starting is highly unreliable, and engine stalls are likely.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_fuel_line_air_leak_stage4_description
text.text: Critical air leak in the fuel line. Fuel supply cannot be maintained and engine start or further operation is not possible.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_electrical_system_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Worn insulation, occasional voltage drops. Minor electrical glitches can be observed.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_electrical_system_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Corroded contacts and unstable alternator output. Lights may fail, engine start is unreliable.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_electrical_system_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Risk of short circuits. Critical systems are unreliable and lights may fail completely.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_electrical_system_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Main wiring harness failed. The vehicle's electrical system is dead.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_carburetor_clogging_stage1_description
text.text: Slight carburetor contamination. Engine occasionally hesitates under heavy load.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_carburetor_clogging_stage2_description
text.text: Clogged jets. Engine frequently sputters and loses power, especially when throttle is applied quickly.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_carburetor_clogging_stage3_description
text.text: Severe carburetor blockage. Engine runs very rough, stalls frequently, and struggles to maintain power.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_carburetor_clogging_stage4_description
text.text: Fuel starvation due to a completely clogged carburetor. The engine will not run.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_harvest_processing_system_wear_stage1_description
text.text: Wear of the harvesting and processing system. Crop processing efficiency is slightly reduced, resulting in minor yield loss.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_harvest_processing_system_wear_stage2_description
text.text: Moderate wear of the harvesting and processing system. Crop processing efficiency is noticeably reduced, resulting in measurable yield loss.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_harvest_processing_system_wear_stage3_description
text.text: Severe wear of the harvesting and processing system. Crop processing efficiency is significantly reduced, resulting in high yield loss.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_harvest_processing_system_wear_stage4_description
text.text: Critical wear of the harvesting and processing system. Crop processing efficiency is critically reduced, causing extreme yield loss during operation.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_material_flow_system_wear_stage1_description
text.text: Slight wear on internal augers and elevator chains. A small amount of crop is lost during internal transport.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_material_flow_system_wear_stage2_description
text.text: Worn auger flights and slipping conveyor belts are causing increased spillage inside the machine. You may hear occasional grinding noises.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_material_flow_system_wear_stage3_description
text.text: Damaged elevator paddles and augers are struggling to move the crop mass, leading to significant losses and a risk of a major clog.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_material_flow_system_wear_stage4_description
text.text: A major component in the material flow system has failed, causing a severe blockage. The engine is under extreme strain.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_unloading_auger_malfunction_stage1_description
text.text: Stretched drive belt. Occasional slipping results in a slight decrease in unloading speed.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_unloading_auger_malfunction_stage2_description
text.text: Worn drive belt and pulleys. Significant slipping under load noticeably reduces unloading speed.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_unloading_auger_malfunction_stage3_description
text.text: Damaged gearbox or heavily worn belt. The auger struggles to turn, operating at a very low speed.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_unloading_auger_malfunction_stage4_description
text.text: Drive belt snapped or gearbox seized. The unloading auger is completely jammed.

text.name: ads_spec_cold_engine_message
text.text: Engine is cold! High revs cause wear!

text.name: ads_spec_next_planned_service_not_enouth_money_notification
text.text: Scheduled %s cancelled due to insufficient funds

text.name: ads_option_menu_inspection_visual_description
text.text: * Quick external inspection. Low cost and immediate result. Detects visible faults, with only a small chance of missing some defects. Does not provide technical condition data.

text.name: ads_option_menu_inspection_standard_description
text.text: * Routine diagnostic procedure. Identifies all active faults and generates a general report on the vehicle's condition.

text.name: ads_option_menu_inspection_complete_description
text.text: * Deep diagnostics. Guarantees detection of all faults, including potential ones, and provides a comprehensive report with exact health percentages for all components.

text.name: ads_option_menu_maintenance_minimal_description
text.text: * Basic service. Significantly cheaper and faster, but does not fully restore the Service Level and provides a shorter service interval until the next maintenance.

text.name: ads_option_menu_maintenance_standard_description
text.text: * Routine maintenance according to manufacturer specifications. Restores Service Level to normal and provides the standard service interval until the next maintenance.

text.name: ads_option_menu_maintenance_extended_description
text.text: * Comprehensive service. Takes longer and costs more, but restores Service Level beyond the standard level and provides a longer service interval until the next maintenance.

text.name: ads_option_menu_maintenance_preventive_description
text.text: * Preventive maintenance beyond standard manufacturer service. Restores Service Level to normal and addresses the most problematic systems to reduce breakdown risk.

text.name: ads_option_menu_repair_type_fix_description
text.text: * Temporary repair intended to keep the machine operational for urgent work. Quickly and inexpensively restores basic functionality, however the fault remains unresolved and will reappear after some time.

text.name: ads_option_menu_repair_type_replacement_description
text.text: * Standard replacement of the failed component. Fully eliminates the current fault and restores the affected unit to normal operating condition.

text.name: ads_option_menu_repair_type_advanced_description
text.text: * In-depth repair including replacement of the failed component and servicing of related parts within the system. Significantly reduces the risk of repeated faults in the affected system.

text.name: ads_option_menu_overhaul_partial_description
text.text: * Rebuilds the selected system to a good operating condition. Faster and less expensive than a full-vehicle overhaul.

text.name: ads_option_menu_overhaul_standard_description
text.text: * Comprehensive work on all major components. Restores Condition to good level.

text.name: ads_option_menu_overhaul_full_description
text.text: * Complete remanufacturing. High cost and duration, but restores Condition to excellent level.

text.name: ads_option_menu_part_used_description
text.text: * Salvaged parts. Very cheap, but extremely risky (~50% chance of defects)

text.name: ads_option_menu_part_aftermarket_description
text.text: * Budget aftermarket copies. Reduced cost, but carries a moderate risk of defects (33%).

text.name: ads_option_menu_part_oem_description
text.text: * Original Equipment Manufacturer parts. Standard reliability (10% defect risk) at full price.

text.name: ads_option_menu_part_premium_description
text.text: * Reinforced performance parts. Expensive, but strictly quality-controlled (0% defect risk).

text.name: ads_option_menu_option_three_disclaimer_repair_after_detection
text.text: * Automatically start standard repair with OEM parts if any faults are detected.

text.name: ads_option_menu_option_three_disclaimer_maintenance_after_repair
text.text: * Automatically start standard maintenance with OEM consumables after repair.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_repair_active_breakdowns
text.text: Active faults detected. Standard repair of all active faults is recommended.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_defective_parts
text.text: Defective replacement parts detected: %s. Preventive replacement is recommended to avoid sudden failure.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_quick_fix
text.text: Temporary repair condition detected: %s. Standard replacement is recommended at the earliest opportunity.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_service_due
text.text: Service interval threshold reached. Perform maintenance.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_service_urgent
text.text: Service interval has been violated. Perform maintenance urgently!

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_overhaul
text.text: Degradation of core components detected. Perform an overhaul.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_system_overhaul
text.text: Critical degradation detected in the %s. Perform a partial overhaul of this system.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_system_preventive_maintenance
text.text: Elevated breakdown risk detected in the %s. Perform preventive maintenance to reduce the risk of failure.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_operating_conditions
text.text: Signs of intensive overuse detected. Improve operating conditions.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_repeat_maintenance
text.text: Defective consumables detected. Repeat maintenance to replace faulty components.

text.name: ads_report_recommendation_all_ok
text.text: No critical recommendations. Vehicle is in serviceable condition.

text.name: ads_air_blower_function
text.text: Used to clean radiators and air intakes

text.name: ads_air_blower_already_clean
text.text: %s: radiator and air intake are already clean

text.name: ads_jumper_cables_function
text.text: Used to to connect vehicle batteries and jump-start dead engines

text.name: ads_jumper_cables_impossible_to_connect
text.text: Vehicle is not suitable for connecting jumper cables

text.name: ads_jumper_cables_both_already_connected
text.text: Jumper cables already connected to %s and %s

text.name: ads_jumper_cables_auto_disconnected
text.text: Jumper cables disconnected because one of the vehicles started moving

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_coolant_leak_stage1
text.text: Slight traces of coolant are visible near the cooling system connections.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_coolant_leak_stage2
text.text: Coolant residue is visible, and the coolant level is below normal.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_coolant_leak_stage3
text.text: Noticeable coolant loss is visible around the cooling system area.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_coolant_leak_stage4
text.text: Coolant level is critically low, with heavy traces of coolant visible.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_oil_pump_malfunction_stage1
text.text: The engine oil looks slightly darkened.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_oil_pump_malfunction_stage2
text.text: The engine oil appears darker than normal.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_hydraulic_cylinder_internal_leak_stage1
text.text: A faint oily film is visible around the hydraulic cylinder area.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_hydraulic_cylinder_internal_leak_stage2
text.text: Hydraulic seepage is visible around the cylinder.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_hydraulic_cylinder_internal_leak_stage3
text.text: Clear hydraulic fluid traces are visible near the cylinder.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_hydraulic_cylinder_internal_leak_stage4
text.text: Heavy hydraulic fluid traces are visible. The cylinder area is heavily contaminated with fluid.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_radiator_clogging_stage1
text.text: The radiator surface is slightly dirty.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_radiator_clogging_stage3
text.text: The radiator is heavily clogged with dirt and debris.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_radiator_clogging_stage4
text.text: The radiator is critically clogged with dirt and debris.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_air_intake_clogging_stage1
text.text: The air intake duct shows light contamination.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_air_intake_clogging_stage2
text.text: The air intake duct is noticeably dirty.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_air_intake_clogging_stage3
text.text: The air intake duct is heavily clogged with dust and debris.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_air_intake_clogging_stage4
text.text: The air intake duct is critically clogged with dust and debris.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_lubrication_stage2
text.text: Lubrication points look dry and insufficiently greased.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_lubrication_stage3
text.text: Lubrication points are very dry, with little visible grease remaining.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_lubrication_stage4
text.text: Lubrication points are critically dry, with almost no visible grease remaining.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_corroded_wiring_stage2
text.text: Light traces of oxidation are visible on some electrical connections.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_corroded_wiring_stage3
text.text: Pronounced oxidation and contamination are visible on the wiring and connectors.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_corroded_wiring_stage4
text.text: Severe corrosion is visible on the wiring and connectors, and some insulation appears damaged.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_battery_sulfation_stage1
text.text: A faint whitish deposit is visible on the battery terminals.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_battery_sulfation_stage2
text.text: A whitish deposit is visible on the battery terminals.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_battery_sulfation_stage3
text.text: Pronounced oxidation and deposits are visible on the battery terminals and casing.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_battery_sulfation_stage4
text.text: The battery and terminals are covered with heavy deposits, and the casing looks dirty and worn.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_alternator_regulator_failure_stage3
text.text: Signs of overheating and darkening are visible around the alternator and some connections.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_alternator_regulator_failure_stage4
text.text: Pronounced signs of overheating and darkened connectors are visible around the alternator and its wiring.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_turbocharger_malfunction_stage3
text.text: Oily traces and darkened connections are visible around the turbocharger area.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_turbocharger_malfunction_stage4
text.text: Heavy oily traces, darkened connections, and soot marks are visible around the turbocharger area.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_powershift_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage3
text.text: Oily traces and contamination are visible around the transmission hydraulic connections.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_powershift_hydraulic_pump_malfunction_stage4
text.text: Heavy oily traces and strong contamination are visible around the transmission hydraulic connections.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_cvt_hydraulic_control_valve_malfunction_stage3
text.text: Oily traces and darkened connections are visible around the CVT hydraulic control block.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_cvt_hydraulic_control_valve_malfunction_stage4
text.text: Heavy oily traces and strong contamination are visible around the CVT hydraulic control block connections.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_track_tensioner_malfunction_stage2
text.text: The track tension appears slightly uneven.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_track_tensioner_malfunction_stage3
text.text: The track appears loose, with noticeable uneven tension.

text.name: ads_inspection_hint_track_tensioner_malfunction_stage4
text.text: The track is visibly sagging, and the tension appears severely compromised.

text.name: ads_ingame_menu_empty_service
text.text: No vehicles are currently in service

text.name: ads_mobile_workshop_low_maintainability
text.text: WARNING: %s is unavailable for %s in the mobile workshop due to low maintainability.

text.name: ads_breakdowns_overload_breakdown_stage2_message
text.text: Critical overload! Reduce the load on the vehicle immediately.

text.name: ads_tutorialMode_tooltip
text.text: Shows tutorial tips during gameplay. Disabled in multiplayer.

text.name: ads_tutorialResetConfirm_message
text.text: Reset tutorial tip history and show all tips again?

text.name: ads_tutorial_welcome_message
text.text:
This mod completely overhauls the vehicle damage and maintenance systems in the game. There are many changes, so tutorial tips will appear from time to time. They will help you understand the new mechanics more quickly, avoid common mistakes, and keep your machines from breaking down.
If you want, you can disable tutorial tips right now. You can always enable them again later in the mod settings, under the Advanced Damage System section.

text.name: ads_tutorial_service_due_soon_message
text.text:
%s will soon need maintenance.
In ADS, vehicles require regular service. If you delay it, the risk of breakdowns rises significantly.
Visit the nearest workshop and select "Maintenance". The service interval can be adjusted in the mod settings.

text.name: ads_tutorial_rad_or_intake_clogged_message
text.text:
Your vehicle’s radiator and air intakes appear to be heavily clogged.
A dirty radiator cannot dissipate heat efficiently, and clogged air intakes can restrict airflow to the engine. This reduces power and increases fuel consumption.
You can clean them at a washing station or with the Air Blower from the shop.
You can check the condition of the radiator and air intakes through inspection: hold the R key while looking at the vehicle.

text.name: ads_tutorial_needs_lubrication_message
text.text:
Your %s needs regular lubrication.
In ADS, the lubrication level of working components gradually drops every day, which increases wear.
Buy a Grease Gun in the shop and lubricate your machine daily or before each work shift.
You can check the lubrication level through inspection: hold the R key while looking at the vehicle.

text.name: ads_tutorial_needs_repair_message
text.text:
It looks like something is wrong with your %s. One of its parts may have failed.
Make sure to visit a workshop and perform an inspection. Do not delay it: the failure may continue to progress, and the repair cost will rise with it.
If the inspection confirms a fault, fix it with the repair procedure at the workshop.

text.name: ads_tutorial_needs_overhaul_message
text.text: It looks like your %s is showing its age. It’s time to consider a replacement or a major overhaul. It’s a long and demanding process, but it’s the best way to give a second life to that favorite vehicle you’ve grown so fond of! P.S. You can change the lifespan in the settings—increasing it will reduce the wear rate of your vehicles.

text.name: ads_tutorial_needs_preventive_message
text.text: It looks like one of %s systems is about to fail. The good news is, you can prevent it! A special service is now available at the workshop: Preventive Maintenance. This includes a full technical inspection plus proactive repairs of any failing components!

text.name: ads_tutorial_poor_consumables_message
text.text:
A little secret: your %s has become a victim of poor-quality consumables during maintenance. That can happen, unless you knowingly chose cheap aftermarket substitutes yourself.
Right now the service interval is shortened, and component wear is slightly increased. A repeat maintenance service will fix the problem. You can identify it through inspection: a basic inspection will warn you about the shortened interval, while a full diagnostic inspection will reveal the poor-quality consumables themselves.

text.name: ads_tutorial_poor_parts_message
text.text:
A little secret: your %s has become a victim of poor-quality parts during repair. That failure will most likely come back again soon.
You can wait for it to happen, or perform a full diagnostic inspection right away — it will reliably reveal the poor-quality part.

text.name: ads_tutorial_idle_and_downtime_message
text.text: By the way, wear is much lower when your machine is running but not actively working, which makes perfect sense. When the engine is off, wear becomes tiny, and if the machine is stored under cover, it disappear completely.

text.name: ads_tutorial_hot_weather_message
text.text:
Well, it is really hot today. In weather like this, you need to watch radiator cleanliness especially closely and avoid overloading the engine — otherwise overheating is only a matter of time.
Remember: you can always check the radiator condition with a visual inspection by holding the R key while looking at the machine, and you can also clean it with the Air Blower.

text.name: ads_tutorial_wet_weather_message
text.text:
What a weather today. Moisture is bad for wiring and electrical equipment, so wear on the electrical system is slightly higher in these conditions.
On top of that, harvesting in weather like this is strongly discouraged — you will only do harm to your combine.

text.name: ads_tutorial_low_fuel_message
text.text: It looks like you are about to run out of fuel. At first glance this may not seem like a major problem, but the less fuel remains in the tank, the harder the fuel pump has to work. Its load increases, and that certainly does not help its service life.

text.name: ads_tutorial_idle_deposit_message
text.text:
It looks like the machine has been idling for too long. Long idle periods are not just wasted fuel: over time they also promote deposits in the fuel system and hurt overall engine health.
If the machine is not needed right now, it is better to shut the engine off.

text.name: ads_tutorial_engine_overheat_message
text.text:
The engine is starting to overheat. This can happen for one or several reasons: the engine is under heavy load, the radiator is clogged, the weather is hot, or there is a fault in the vehicle's cooling system.
Stay alert: overheating is one of the most destructive factors for any machine. It does not only harm the engine: the cooling system is pushed to its limit, and heat in the engine bay accelerates the degradation of wiring and electrical components.

text.name: ads_tutorial_cvt_overheat_message
text.text:
The CVT is starting to overheat. This can happen for one or several reasons: the transmission is under excessive load, the radiator is clogged, or the weather is very hot. For example, you may be pulling a plow that is too heavy or towing an overloaded trailer.
Stay alert: overheating is one of the most destructive things that can happen to a CVT transmission.

text.name: ads_tutorial_heavy_trailer_message
text.text:
It looks like you are trying to tow a trailer that is too heavy for your tractor. You can keep doing it, but the transmission will be under enormous stress, and the brakes will not appreciate the extra load either.
Follow a simple rule: 10 HP or more per 1 ton of towed weight, and the machine will last noticeably longer.

text.name: ads_tutorial_chassis_vibration_message
text.text: Whoa, slow down! Driving fast over rough terrain will quickly wear out the suspension of your %s. On top of that, those vibrations also harm other systems — especially hydraulics and electrics. Drive smoothly and take it easy.

text.name: ads_tutorial_steering_message
text.text:
It is best not to steer while standing still. This puts a heavy load on the steering gear, power steering system, and front axle components, which increases wear on the running gear.
Just start moving at least a little, and the wheels will turn much more easily.

text.name: ads_tutorial_cranking_message
text.text:
It looks like you are starting the engine too often. Every engine start sends a very high current through the electrical system, which accelerates wear on the wiring, starter, and other electrical components.
Try not to crank the starter too often and do not shut the engine off as if the machine had a start-stop system.

text.name: ads_tutorial_hard_start_message
text.text: Starting the engine on the %s is difficult. This may be caused by a low battery, freezing temperatures, mechanical faults, or starter wear. To start the engine, hold down the ignition button. Note: The electrical system experiences heavy loads during startup due to high inrush currents!

text.name: ads_tutorial_critical_failure_message
text.text:
Well, that is bad luck. Your %s is completely disabled and can no longer move under its own power. You can use a mobile workshop and perform a quick repair so the vehicle can make it to the workshop on its own.
Otherwise, you will have to come up with some other way to recover it.

text.name: ads_tutorial_heavy_lift_message
text.text:
It looks like this implement is too heavy for your %s. The hydraulics are working at their limit, which will negatively affect their service life.
Follow a simple rule: the attachment weight should not exceed 60%% of the vehicle's own weight.

text.name: ads_tutorial_cold_engine_message
text.text:
Always warm up your machine before putting it to work. Besides the engine, there are many other parts that need time to warm up as well: hydraulic oil, the CVT transmission, and even the fuel temperature — especially in freezing weather. Running the machine cold can cause problems across multiple systems.
If the engine temperature is above 50 degrees, everything is warmed up properly and the machine can be used safely.

text.name: ads_tutorial_pto_sharp_angle_message
text.text:
Be careful: the PTO angle between your machine and the implement is too large. The driveshaft starts working with harsh pulsations, vibration increases, and wear builds up quickly.
Keep the angle below 30 degrees and try to disengage the PTO during sharp turns and headland maneuvers.

text.name: ads_tutorial_engine_overload_message
text.text:
The engine is under enormous strain. It may be that the task you are trying to do is simply too much for this machine, and you should consider using a more powerful unit. Or it may be enough to slow down and reduce the load on the engine.
In any case, keep in mind that wear rises noticeably under this kind of stress.

text.name: ads_tutorial_lugging_message
text.text: The engine, and especially the transmission, do not like lugging: low RPM under heavy engine load. You may have attached something too heavy for the machine, or you may simply be choosing poor gears if the gearbox is manual.

text.name: ads_tutorial_wheel_slip_message
text.text: It looks like your wheels are slipping badly. You may even be stuck right now and not in the mood for advice, but keep this in mind: wheel slip is one of the most destructive factors for the transmission and will bring your next service visit much closer.

text.name: ads_tutorial_battery_low_message
text.text:
Oops, it looks like the battery is about to die. This can happen after a long period of standing, because of severe cold, or due to faults in the electrical system.
With a low charge, starting will be difficult or even impossible. If you cannot get it started, you can jump-start the machine from another vehicle using Jumper Cables.
